Question: Water towers store water above the level of consumers for times of heavy use, eliminating the need for high-speed pumps. How high above a user must the water level be to create a gauge pressure of $$3.00 \times 10^{5} \text{N/m}^{2}$$ ?
o3 Answer:
For a static column of fluid the gauge pressure produced at a depth h is  

P = ρ g h

where  
ρ = density of water ≈ 1.0 × 10³ kg/m³  
g = 9.80 m/s²  
P = 3.00 × 10⁵ N/m²

Solve for h:

h = P / (ρ g)  
  = (3.00 × 10⁵ N/m²)/(1.0 × 10³ kg/m³ × 9.80 m/s²)  
  = 3.00 × 10⁵ / 9.80 × 10³  
  ≈ 30.6 m

Therefore, the water surface must be roughly 31 m (about 100 ft) above the consumer to provide the desired gauge pressure.

Question: Bird bones have air pockets in them to reduce their weight-this also gives them an average density significantly less than that of the bones of other animals. Suppose an ornithologist weighs a bird bone in air and in water and finds its mass is 45.0 g and its apparent mass when submerged is 3.60 g (assume the bone is watertight). (a) What mass of water is displaced? (b) What is the volume of the bone? (c) What is its average density?
Answer: a. 41.4 g; b. 41.4 cm^3; c. 1.09 g/cm^3. This is clearly not the density of the bone everywhere. The air pockets will have a density of approximately $$1.29 \times 10^{-3} \text{g/cm}^{3}$$ , while the bone will be substantially denser.

m​ = true mass of the bone (weighed in air) = 45.0 g  
m’ = apparent mass when the bone is completely submerged in water = 3.60 g  

When an object is submerged, the scale reading is reduced by the buoyant force, whose magnitude equals the weight of the water displaced:

m g – ρwater V g = m’ g

where V is the volume of the bone and ρwater = 1.00 g cm⁻³.  
Rearranging,

ρwater V = m – m’        (1)

(a) Mass of water displaced  
mwater = m – m’ = 45.0 g – 3.60 g = 41.4 g

(b) Volume of the bone  
Because ρwater = 1.00 g cm⁻³, each gram of water occupies 1.00 cm³.  
Hence

V = mwater / ρwater = 41.4 g / (1.00 g cm⁻³) = 41.4 cm³  
= 4.14 × 10⁻⁵ m³ (if SI units are desired).

(c) Average density of the bone  
ρbone = m / V = 45.0 g / 41.4 cm³ = 1.09 g cm⁻³  
= 1.09 × 10³ kg m⁻³

So,

(a) 41.4 g of water is displaced.  
(b) The bone’s volume is 41.4 cm³ (4.14 × 10⁻⁵ m³).  
(c) Its average density is about 1.09 g cm⁻³ (≈ 1.09 × 10³ kg m⁻³).
